items.title: ‘She wants him gone’: My friend took in a homeless man as a caretaker. After 10 years, how can she evict him?
items.description: “He lived rent-free, helping her with health-related issues and other tasks.”

items.title: Almost all of the Nasdaq-100’s gains in the first half of 2026 came from just 10 stocks
items.description: Micron led the way, contributing 26% of the Nasdaq-100’s first-half returns and 17% of the S&P 500’s gains, according to Jefferies.

items.title: ServiceNow and Salesforce shares now look like buys, as ‘Armageddon’ fears are too extreme, analyst says
items.description: A Guggenheim analyst says that valuations for the two software stocks are too depressed, even though the AI threat is real.

items.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to recognize first.
items.description: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

items.title: ChatGPT is ready to connect to your bank accounts. Should you let it in?
items.description: OpenAI is letting users link their bank accounts to its chatbot, but cybersecurity experts have safety concerns. Here’s what you should know.

items.title: If LeBron James goes to the Warriors, that team’s roster will be stacked with some of the most expensive players in NBA history
items.description: LeBron James’s agent has told the Los Angeles Lakers that he intends to sign with a new team, according to a new report.

items.title: My student loans are paused until 2028. Should I pay them now anyway?
items.description: Plus: What’s going on with ‘grade inflation’?

items.title: Record beef imports are flooding the U.S. — so why is your Fourth of July BBQ so expensive?
items.description: Beef prices are sizzling and Washington’s recipe for high costs — import more meat — only grills your wallet.

items.title: Businesses added 98,000 jobs in June, ADP says. Labor market is on the mend, but it’s still not great.
items.description: ADP said U.S. businesses 98,000 new jobs in June — the smallest increase in three months. The  payroll processor said hiring was slow but demand for labor was improving.

items.title: Warsh warns investors not to expect hints at future Fed rate moves
items.description: New Federal Reserve chief Kevin Warsh gave no hint of whether the central bank would raise interest rates in July and repeated his dislike of so-called forward guidance to alert investors to future Fe
